case_name: Baylor Scott & White Health, and Scott & White Healthcare v. Jerry Bostick

summary.brief: Baylor Scott & White Health, and Scott & White Healthcare v. Jerry Bostick. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Bostick brought this suit to recover for injuries he sustained from a fall at a BSW hospital in Marble Falls, Texas.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Finding that Bostick was an invitee and BSW was ninety-four percent responsible for causing or contributing to his injuries, the jury returned a verdict in Bostick’s favor for $2,470,000.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Bostick, following the ambulance, drove Selman’s wife to the hospital. They checked in at a reception booth, and a nurse led them to Selman in the treatment area through two sets of automatic doors.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Bostick raised his hand to get the pastor’s attention, and one door started to close against him. He pulled the door 'back a little tug,' but it continued to move toward him, hit him on his left side, and knocked him to the floor. Bostick suffered a broken hip.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We reverse the trial court’s judgment and remand for a new trial on Bostick’s premises liability claim against BSW.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Having concluded that the trial court erroneously charged the jury with an incorrect legal standard for determining liability and the error was harmful, we reverse the trial court’s judgment and remand the case for a new trial on Bostick’s premises liability claim against BSW.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was visiting a hospital and was injured when an automatic door closed on him, causing a fall.

focused_exam.musculoskeletal: Patient suffered a broken hip from the fall

requestables.name: X-ray of the hip
requestables.description: Imaging to assess the extent of hip injury

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Visit
timeline_phases.description: Patient visits hospital and sustains injury

decision_points.prompt: What should be done to ensure patient safety in the hospital environment?

options.description: Ensure all automatic doors have safety sensors
options.clinical_reasoning: Prevents similar incidents by ensuring doors do not close on individuals

options.description: Rely on manual operation of doors by staff
options.clinical_reasoning: Relies on human intervention, which can be inconsistent

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant relied on manual operation of doors

expected_action_court.description: Automatic doors should have safety sensors

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Manual operation is not reliable for ensuring safety
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Automatic doors should have been equipped with safety sensors
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Premises liability for ensuring safe environment

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered a broken hip requiring medical intervention

key_findings: Breach of duty in ensuring safe premises
Causation established - failure to prevent door accident

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to ensure automatic door safety
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Safety sensors would have prevented the accident
